Identity.Name: StingObservationLOS
Identity.Definition: Patient observation line-of-sight classification per NHS England Mental Health Premises Standards. Carried on patient bedrooms / dayrooms / circulation in mental-health units. Drives the observation-line validator + staff-base visibility analysis, plus the room-by-room observation-grade dashboard.

Governance.StandardsBasis: HBN 03-01:2013; NHS England Mental Health Premises Standards 2022; CQC observation guidance

Value.Definition: Direct — uninterrupted line of sight from a staff observation point to the entire room. Highest observation grade; required for high-risk patient areas (acute admission, intensive psychiatric care).
Indirect — observation via mirror, dome, CCTV, or staffed walkway. Acceptable for medium-risk patient areas with documented mitigation.
Intermittent — observation via periodic rounds (typically 15-minute or 30-minute intervals). Used for stable patient areas.
None — no observation requirement. Bathroom interiors, en-suite cubicles (privacy/dignity priority), low-risk dayrooms with self-management.
Continuous — 1:1 / 2:1 staffing with constant staff presence in or at the door of the room. Highest-risk patient observation. No technical room provision (people-based).
